title: agropyron repens var. geniculatum
intro: Agropyron repens var. geniculatum, also known as creeping bentgrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the wheat grass family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of creeping bentgrass include its ability to soothe the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and improve overall gut health. Herbal preparations of creeping bentgrass can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
